content.claim: Hungary exhibits extreme school segregation in small settlements, with disadvantaged-pupil concentration ranging from 20% to 80% within the same locality, shaped by covert admissions practices, church-school choice, and parental mobility responses to changing school composition.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Segregation research cited is not always SEN-specific; most sources focus on Roma/socioeconomic segregation rather than SEN-driven mobility
One claim about parents withdrawing children when SNI concentration reaches 20–40% is presented as an advocacy hypothesis rather than an empirically validated causal finding
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
